headline: Geopolitical tensions split AI models on broad market direction
summary: Four AI models made 16 predictions today amid significant geopolitical escalation that spiked oil prices and triggered flight-to-safety buying. A clear consensus emerged around safe-haven assets (gold and oil) with high confidence, while the broader market direction remained deeply contested—Claude and Grok favored upside despite headwinds, while Perplexity called a sharp selloff. Results remain pending as actual closing prices have not yet been recorded.

consensus_picks.note: Strongest consensus of the day with 0.82 and 0.70 confidence—gold's safe-haven status overwhelmingly supported.
Major disagreement with GPT-4o and Grok bullish while Perplexity bearish at 0.72 confidence on geopolitical headwinds.
Both models cited tech sector vulnerability with Perplexity's 1.6% futures drop more severe than Claude's 1.5% premarket decline.
Claude bullish on tax credit news (0.60 confidence) while GPT-4o bearish on regulatory scrutiny (0.60 confidence)—conflicting catalysts.

best_call.summary: Perplexity's crude oil call with 0.78 confidence and 13% WTI spike reasoning demonstrated superior geopolitical event analysis and commodity trading logic.

worst_call.summary: GPT-4o's bearish TSLA target of $750 contradicted same-day bullish momentum from federal tax credits and conflicted with market reality showing 5.3% recent gains.
